rule: IPO can skip the normal three-full-calendar-month seasoning rule if its Full Market Capitalization ranks within the top 40 current Nasdaq-100 constituents. It is evaluated after the 7th trading day and typically added after 15 trading days, with announcement after the 10th trading day.
spacex_read: Likely eligible if SPCX lists on an eligible Nasdaq market and no final filing changes the share count. At $1.77T basic market cap, the top-40 size test should not be the gating item.
first_6m_model_impact: Creates a near-term QQQ/NDX flow catalyst around late June to early July 2026, not a day-one inclusion.

rule: Standard liquidity screen is three-month ADVT of at least $5M. For IPO fast entry, ADVT is measured from first trading day through the applicable reference date.
spacex_read: A $75B IPO should almost certainly clear $5M ADVT, but this cannot be final until trading occurs.
first_6m_model_impact: Liquidity is a check-the-box risk, not the main risk.

rule: No minimum free-float criterion. For low-float securities, index weighting uses the lesser of reported TSO or 3x free-floating shares.
spacex_read: Base IPO float is 555.6M shares, or 4.25% of basic shares. Initial NDX modified market cap is about $225.0B, not the full $1765.2B company value.
first_6m_model_impact: The rule allows inclusion but scales the starting weight to tradable supply. This reduces, but does not eliminate, forced-buy pressure.

rule: The updated framework adds rank-based quarterly reviews in March, June and September, plus the annual December reconstitution.
spacex_read: If fast entry were missed for an operational reason, the next scheduled framework matters. The first quarterly rebalance under the new rules is June 22, 2026.
first_6m_model_impact: Index demand can arrive in tranches rather than only at December reconstitution.

rule: IPOs should trade on an eligible exchange for at least 12 months before consideration for S&P Composite 1500 inclusion. No IPO fast track is allowed for S&P Composite 1500 candidates.
spacex_read: Assuming first trade on June 12, 2026, the earliest simple seasoning date is June 12, 2027. This is only a possible consideration date, not an inclusion guarantee.
first_6m_model_impact: No S&P 500 forced-buy catalyst in the first six months.
source: S&P U.S. Indices Methodology; S&P MegaCap consultation results

rule: GAAP net income from continuing operations must be positive for the most recent quarter and for the sum of the most recent four consecutive quarters.
spacex_read: SpaceX reported a 2025 net loss and a Q1 2026 net loss. It does not currently clear the profitability screen.
first_6m_model_impact: Even after 12 months, S&P 500 inclusion likely requires a demonstrated profit turn.
source: S&P U.S. Indices Methodology; S&P MegaCap consultation results

rule: S&P Composite 1500 additions require IWF of at least 0.10, security-level FMC of at least 50% of the index market-cap threshold, FALR of at least 0.75, and at least 250,000 shares traded in each of the six months before evaluation.
spacex_read: Base IPO float is only 4.25% of basic shares, below the 10% IWF screen if only IPO shares count. Lock-up releases could change this later.
first_6m_model_impact: Float unlocks matter for S&P eligibility, but they also create supply risk.
source: S&P U.S. Indices Methodology; S&P MegaCap consultation results

index: S&P Total Market / Dow Jones U.S. Total Stock Market
rule: Effective June 8, 2026, broad-market indices allow an alternative IWF path for mega-cap companies and preserve IPO fast-track treatment when all other criteria are met.
spacex_read: Base float-adjusted market cap is about $75.0B, so broad-index eligibility may arrive much earlier than S&P 500 eligibility.
first_6m_model_impact: Potential total-market ETF flow is relevant, but it should not be confused with S&P 500 membership.
